items.text:
Hi Priya,

Just a reminder: the required lab safety training is this Thursday, March 12, at 3:00 PM in Chem Building Room 108. Bring your $5 safety goggles deposit in cash, since the equipment desk can't process cards that day.

Thanks,
Mr. Delgado

questions.stem: According to the email, when is the lab safety training scheduled?
questions.explanation: The email states the training "is this Thursday, March 12, at 3:00 PM," matching option B.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about paying for the safety goggles deposit?

options.A: Payment can be completed online in advance.
options.B: Payment must be refunded before the session.
options.C: Payment must be made in cash that day.
options.D: Payment can be postponed until the following week.

questions.explanation: Since the equipment desk "can't process cards that day," the $5 deposit must be paid in cash on-site.

items.text:
Hi Marcus,

Quick heads-up: this week's Hiking Club meeting has moved from Tuesday to Friday, April 17, at 6:00 PM in Founders Hall, Room 220, since the auditorium is booked. We're finalizing the Cedar Ridge trip route, so please try to attend if you can.

Best,
Nora

format_metadata.subject: Hiking Club meeting moved to Friday

questions.stem: According to the email, where and when will this week's Hiking Club meeting take place?

options.A: Tuesday, 6:00 PM, in Founders Hall, Room 220
options.C: Friday, 4:00 PM, in Founders Hall, Room 220
options.D: Friday, 6:00 PM, in Founders Hall, Room 220

questions.explanation: The email states the meeting "has moved from Tuesday to Friday, April 17, at 6:00 PM in Founders Hall, Room 220."

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the reason for the schedule change?

options.A: The auditorium was already reserved that day.
options.B: The club president resigned earlier this week.
options.C: Too few members signed up on Tuesday.
options.D: The Cedar Ridge trip was fully canceled.

questions.explanation: The email explains the move happened "since the auditorium is booked," implying the usual space was already reserved.

items.text: CAMPUS PHOTO CONTEST! Submit up to three photos on the theme "Everyday Wonder" by Friday, September 25, 11:59 PM. Entry form available at the Student Union, Room 150. First prize: a $200 gift card and a feature in the fall arts magazine. Open to all students!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what does the first-place winner receive?

options.A: A $150 gift card and a magazine feature
options.B: A $200 gift card and a website mention
options.C: A $200 certificate and a magazine feature
options.D: A $200 gift card and a magazine feature

questions.explanation: The flyer states first prize is "a $200 gift card and a feature in the fall arts magazine."

questions.stem: What can be inferred about participating in the contest?

options.A: Students must obtain an entry form first.
options.B: Students must pay a fee to enter.
options.C: Students must attend an orientation session.
options.D: Students must major in photography courses.

questions.explanation: Since the flyer notes the "entry form available at the Student Union, Room 150," students must get that form before submitting photos.

items.text:
Hi Owen,

Your replacement student ID is ready for pickup at the Registrar's Office, Room 105, before 4:30 PM on Wednesday, October 8. Please bring a $10 processing fee in exact cash, as the office can't issue change.

Thanks,
Ms. Ferraro

format_metadata.subject: Replacement student ID ready for pickup

questions.stem: According to the email, by when must Owen pick up his replacement ID?
questions.explanation: The email states the ID is ready for pickup "before 4:30 PM on Wednesday, October 8."

questions.stem: What can be inferred about paying the $10 processing fee?

options.A: Payment can be made with a credit card.
options.B: Payment can be deferred until next week.
options.C: Payment must be exact cash, no change.
options.D: Payment must be made online in advance.

questions.explanation: Since the office "can't issue change," Owen must bring exact cash for the $10 fee.
